Title: How to enroll in health insurance — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does an employee in Engineering enroll in the company health insurance plan?

## Answer

Employees can enroll in health insurance during open enrollment (November 1-30) or within 30 days of a qualifying life event.

### Enrollment Steps

1. Log in to the Benefits Portal at benefits.company.com
2. Click "Benefits Enrollment" from the dashboard
3. Select "Health Insurance" from the available benefit categories
4. Review the plan options:
   - **Basic Plan**: $150/month employee-only, $400/month family
   - **Standard Plan**: $250/month employee-only, $600/month family
   - **Premium Plan**: $375/month employee-only, $900/month family
5. Select your preferred plan and coverage level
6. Add dependents if applicable (spouse, children under 26)
7. Upload required documentation (marriage certificate for spouse, birth certificates for children)
8. Review and confirm your selections
9. Submit enrollment

### Important Notes

- Coverage begins the 1st of the month following enrollment
- Changes outside open enrollment require a qualifying life event (marriage, birth, adoption, loss of other coverage)
- Contact HR Benefits Team at benefits@company.com for questions
Author: HR Operations

Title: How to update beneficiary information — new hire
Content:
## Question

An employee in Engineering needs to update their beneficiary designations. What is the process?

## Answer

Beneficiary information should be reviewed and updated whenever a major life event occurs (marriage, divorce, birth of child, death of beneficiary).

### Steps to Update

1. Log in to the Benefits Portal at benefits.company.com
2. Navigate to "My Benefits" > "Life Insurance & AD&D"
3. Click "Update Beneficiaries"
4. For each policy (Basic Life, Supplemental Life, AD&D):
   - Enter beneficiary name, relationship, date of birth, and SSN
   - Assign percentage allocation (must total 100%)
   - Designate as Primary or Contingent beneficiary
5. Review all entries for accuracy
6. E-sign the beneficiary designation form
7. Submit changes

### Important Notes

- Changes take effect immediately upon submission
- It is recommended to name both primary and contingent beneficiaries
- Minor children cannot directly receive life insurance proceeds — consider naming a trust
- If no beneficiary is on file, proceeds go to the employee's estate
- Keep a personal copy of your beneficiary designations
Author: Talent Acquisition

Beneficiary information should be reviewed and updated whenever a major life event occurs (marriage, divorce, birth of child, death of beneficiary).

### Steps to Update

1. Log in to the Benefits Portal at benefits.company.com
2. Navigate to "My Benefits" > "Life Insurance & AD&D"
3. Click "Update Beneficiaries"
4. For each policy (Basic Life, Supplemental Life, AD&D):
   - Enter beneficiary name, relationship, date of birth, and SSN
   - Assign percentage allocation (must total 100%)
   - Designate as Primary or Contingent beneficiary
5. Review all entries for accuracy
6. E-sign the beneficiary designation form
7. Submit changes

### Important Notes

- Changes take effect immediately upon submission
- It is recommended to name both primary and contingent beneficiaries
- Minor children cannot directly receive life insurance proceeds — consider naming a trust
- If no beneficiary is on file, proceeds go to the employee's estate
- Keep a personal copy of your beneficiary designations
Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Understanding the 401(k) retirement plan — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What are the details of the company 401(k) retirement plan for Engineering employees?

## Answer

All full-time employees are eligible for the company 401(k) plan after 90 days of employment.

### Plan Details

- **Employee Contributions**: 1% to 75% of eligible compensation (pre-tax or Roth)
- **Company Match**: 100% match on first 3%, then 50% on next 2% (max 4% company contribution)
- **Vesting Schedule**: Company match vests over 3 years (33% per year)
- **Annual Limit**: $23,500 for 2026 ($31,000 if age 50+)

### How to Enroll or Change Contributions

1. Log in to the retirement portal at retire.company.com
2. Click "Manage Contributions"
3. Set your contribution percentage
4. Select pre-tax, Roth, or a combination
5. Choose your investment allocations from available funds
6. Confirm and submit

### Automatic Enrollment

- New hires are auto-enrolled at 3% pre-tax after 90 days
- Contributions auto-escalate 1% annually up to 10% unless opted out

### Contact

- Plan Administrator: Fidelity Investments (800-555-0199)
- HR Benefits Team: benefits@company.com
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: How to file a workers compensation claim — new hire
Content:
## Question

An employee in Engineering was injured on the job. How do they file a workers' compensation claim?

## Answer

All work-related injuries must be reported immediately, regardless of severity.

### Immediate Steps

1. Seek medical attention if needed — for emergencies call 911
2. Notify your direct supervisor as soon as possible
3. Report the injury to HR within 24 hours

### Filing the Claim

1. Complete the Incident Report Form (available on HR Portal > Forms > Safety)
2. Provide details: date, time, location, description of injury, witnesses
3. Submit the form to HR Operations at hr-safety@company.com
4. HR will file the claim with the workers' comp insurance carrier within 48 hours
5. You will receive a claim number and adjuster contact information

### Medical Treatment

- Use an approved workers' comp physician for initial treatment
- List of approved providers available on HR Portal > Workers Comp > Providers
- Keep all medical records and receipts
- Follow all prescribed treatment plans

### Return to Work

- A physician's release is required before returning to work
- Modified duty may be available depending on restrictions
- HR will coordinate a return-to-work plan with your manager
Author: Payroll Team

1. Complete the Incident Report Form (available on HR Portal > Forms > Safety)
2. Provide details: date, time, location, description of injury, witnesses
3. Submit the form to HR Operations at hr-safety@company.com
4. HR will file the claim with the workers' comp insurance carrier within 48 hours
5. You will receive a claim number and adjuster contact information

### Medical Treatment

- Use an approved workers' comp physician for initial treatment
- List of approved providers available on HR Portal > Workers Comp > Providers
- Keep all medical records and receipts
- Follow all prescribed treatment plans

### Return to Work

- A physician's release is required before returning to work
- Modified duty may be available depending on restrictions
- HR will coordinate a return-to-work plan with your manager

Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: How to opt into the employee stock purchase plan — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How can Engineering employees participate in the Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)?

## Answer

The ESPP allows eligible employees to purchase company stock at a 15% discount through payroll deductions.

### Eligibility

- Must be a full-time employee (30+ hours/week)
- Must have completed 6 months of employment
- Cannot own more than 5% of company stock

### Enrollment Periods

- Two offering periods per year: January-June and July-December
- Enrollment windows open 2 weeks before each period starts

### How to Enroll

1. Log in to the ESPP portal at equity.company.com
2. Click "Enroll in ESPP"
3. Set your payroll deduction (1% to 15% of base salary)
4. Review and accept the plan terms
5. Submit enrollment

### How It Works

- Payroll deductions accumulate over the 6-month offering period
- At the end of the period, shares are purchased at 15% below the lower of the start or end price
- Shares are deposited into your brokerage account within 5 business days
- You can sell shares anytime after purchase (tax implications apply)

### Tax Considerations

- Consult the ESPP Tax Guide on the HR Portal
- Qualifying dispositions require holding shares for 2+ years from offering date
- Disqualifying dispositions are taxed as ordinary income

Title: Dental insurance plan options — new hire
Content:
## Question

What dental insurance options are available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company offers two dental plan options through Delta Dental.

### Plan Comparison

| Feature | Basic Dental | Premium Dental |
|---------|-------------|----------------|
| Monthly Premium (Employee) | $25 | $55 |
| Monthly Premium (Family) | $75 | $150 |
| Annual Maximum | $1,500 | $3,000 |
| Deductible | $50 individual / $150 family | $25 individual / $75 family |
| Preventive (cleanings, x-rays) | 100% covered | 100% covered |

### Enrollment

1. Enroll during open enrollment or within 30 days of hire
2. Access the Benefits Portal > Dental Insurance
3. Select your plan and coverage tier

Author: Payroll Team

Title: Vision insurance coverage details — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What does the company vision insurance plan cover for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Vision insurance is provided through VSP Vision Care.

### Coverage Details

- **Monthly Premium**: $10 employee / $25 family
- **Eye Exam**: $10 copay, covered once every 12 months
- **Prescription Glasses**:
  - Frames: $150 allowance every 24 months
  - Lenses: $25 copay, covered every 12 months
  - Lens enhancements (anti-reflective, progressive): 20-30% discount
- **Contact Lenses**: $150 allowance every 12 months (in lieu of glasses)
- **Laser Vision Correction**: 15% discount or 5% off promotional pricing

### How to Use Your Benefits

1. Find a VSP provider at vsp.com/find-a-doctor
2. Tell the provider you have VSP coverage
3. Provide your member ID (found on Benefits Portal)
4. Pay your copay at time of service
5. VSP handles the rest directly with the provider

### Out-of-Network Reimbursement

- Eye exam: up to $50
- Frames: up to $70
- Lenses: up to $50
- Contacts: up to $105
- Submit claims within 6 months at vsp.com

Author: HR Generalist

Title: How to request a compensation review — new hire
Content:
## Question

An employee in Engineering wants to request a review of their compensation. What is the process?

## Answer

Compensation reviews can be requested outside of the annual merit cycle under certain circumstances.

### When to Request a Review

- Significant change in job responsibilities
- Market data suggests current pay is below range
- Completion of a relevant certification or degree
- Promotion or reclassification of role
- Retention concern (do not use as a counter-offer tool)

### Process

1. Discuss your request with your direct manager first
2. Manager submits a Compensation Review Request form via the HR Portal
3. Form requires: current salary, proposed adjustment, justification, market data
4. HR Compensation Analyst reviews the request against:
   - Internal equity (similar roles, experience levels)
   - External market data (salary surveys, industry benchmarks)
   - Budget availability
   - Performance history
5. HR provides recommendation to manager within 10 business days
6. Manager and HR discuss and finalize decision
7. Employee is notified of the outcome

### Annual Merit Cycle

- Occurs in March each year
- Based on performance rating and budget allocation
- Managers receive merit guidelines from HR in February
- Adjustments effective April 1
Author: HR Generalist

Title: HSA and FSA account information — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What are the differences between HSA and FSA accounts, and how do Engineering employees enroll?

## Answer

Both Health Savings Accounts (HSA) and Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) let you set aside pre-tax dollars for medical expenses.

### HSA (Health Savings Account)

- **Eligibility**: Must be enrolled in the High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP)
- **2026 Contribution Limits**: $4,300 individual / $8,550 family (+$1,000 catch-up if 55+)
- **Company Contribution**: $500 individual / $1,000 family annually
- **Rollover**: Funds roll over year to year — no use-it-or-lose-it
- **Portability**: Account stays with you if you leave the company
- **Investment**: Balances over $1,000 can be invested in mutual funds

### FSA (Flexible Spending Account)

- **Eligibility**: All benefits-eligible employees (cannot have both HSA and Healthcare FSA)
- **2026 Contribution Limit**: $3,300
- **Rollover**: Up to $640 rolls over; remaining balance forfeited
- **Dependent Care FSA**: Separate $5,000 limit for childcare/eldercare expenses

### Enrollment

1. Log in to Benefits Portal during open enrollment
2. Select HSA or FSA (based on your health plan)
3. Enter your annual election amount
4. Funds are available:
   - HSA: as contributed each paycheck
   - FSA: full annual amount available January 1

### Using Your Account

- HSA/FSA debit card for point-of-sale purchases
- Submit receipts for reimbursement via the benefits app
- Keep all receipts for tax documentation
Author: HR Operations

- **2026 Contribution Limit**: $3,300
- **Rollover**: Up to $640 rolls over; remaining balance forfeited
- **Dependent Care FSA**: Separate $5,000 limit for childcare/eldercare expenses

### Enrollment

1. Log in to Benefits Portal during open enrollment
2. Select HSA or FSA (based on your health plan)
3. Enter your annual election amount
4. Funds are available:
   - HSA: as contributed each paycheck
   - FSA: full annual amount available January 1

### Using Your Account

- HSA/FSA debit card for point-of-sale purchases
- Submit receipts for reimbursement via the benefits app
- Keep all receipts for tax documentation

Title: Life insurance and AD&D coverage — new hire
Content:
## Question

What life insurance and AD&D benefits are available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company provides both basic (company-paid) and voluntary (employee-paid) life insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) coverage.

### Basic Life Insurance (Company-Paid)

- **Coverage**: 1x annual salary (up to $500,000)
- **Cost**: Fully paid by employer
- **Eligibility**: All full-time employees from date of hire

### Voluntary Life Insurance (Employee-Paid)

- **Employee Coverage**: Up to 5x annual salary (max $1,000,000)
- **Spouse Coverage**: Up to $250,000
- **Child Coverage**: $10,000 per child
- **Guaranteed Issue**: Up to 3x salary if enrolled within 30 days of hire (no medical questions)
- **Evidence of Insurability**: Required for amounts over guaranteed issue

### AD&D Coverage

- **Basic AD&D**: Equal to basic life insurance amount (company-paid)
- **Voluntary AD&D**: Available in increments of $50,000 up to $500,000
- **Covers**: Accidental death, loss of limb, loss of sight, paralysis

### Monthly Cost Examples (per $10,000 of coverage)

| Age Band | Employee Rate |
|----------|-------------|
| Under 30 | $0.05 |
| 30-34 | $0.06 |
| 35-39 | $0.08 |
| 40-44 | $0.12 |
| 45-49 | $0.20 |
| 50-54 | $0.35 |
| 55-59 | $0.55 |
| 60+ | $0.95 |

### Enrollment

- Enroll via Benefits Portal during open enrollment or within 30 days of hire
- Beneficiary designations required — update after any life event
Author: Learning & Development

Title: How to request PTO — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does an employee in Engineering request paid time off (PTO)?

## Answer

All PTO requests are submitted through the Time & Attendance system.

### Steps to Request PTO

1. Log in to timeoff.company.com
2. Click "New Time Off Request"
3. Select the type of leave:
   - **Vacation**: Standard PTO
   - **Personal Day**: For personal matters
   - **Floating Holiday**: Company-designated flexible holidays
4. Enter the dates requested
5. Add a brief note (optional but recommended for extended absences)
6. Submit the request

### Approval Process

- Requests go to your direct manager for approval
- Managers should respond within 2 business days
- For requests of 5+ consecutive days, submit at least 2 weeks in advance
- Peak blackout periods may apply depending on Engineering department needs

### PTO Accrual Rates

| Years of Service | Annual PTO Days |
|-----------------|----------------|
| 0-2 years | 15 days |
| 3-5 years | 20 days |
| 6-10 years | 25 days |
| 11+ years | 30 days |

### Important Policies

- Maximum carryover: 5 days into the next year
- PTO is paid out upon separation (up to accrued balance)
- Negative PTO balances are not permitted
Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: How to request PTO — international employee
Content:
## Question

How does an employee in Logistics request paid time off (PTO)?

## Answer

All PTO requests are submitted through the Time & Attendance system.

### Steps to Request PTO

1. Log in to timeoff.company.com
2. Click "New Time Off Request"
3. Select the type of leave:
   - **Vacation**: Standard PTO
   - **Personal Day**: For personal matters
   - **Floating Holiday**: Company-designated flexible holidays
4. Enter the dates requested
5. Add a brief note (optional but recommended for extended absences)
6. Submit the request

### Approval Process

- Requests go to your direct manager for approval
- Managers should respond within 2 business days
- For requests of 5+ consecutive days, submit at least 2 weeks in advance
- Peak blackout periods may apply depending on Logistics department needs

### PTO Accrual Rates

| Years of Service | Annual PTO Days |
|-----------------|----------------|
| 0-2 years | 15 days |
| 3-5 years | 20 days |
| 6-10 years | 25 days |
| 11+ years | 30 days |

### Important Policies

- Maximum carryover: 5 days into the next year
- PTO is paid out upon separation (up to accrued balance)
- Negative PTO balances are not permitted

Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: FMLA eligibility and application process — new hire
Content:
## Question

An employee in Engineering needs to take FMLA leave. What are the requirements and how do they apply?

## Answer

The Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) provides eligible employees up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave per year.

### Eligibility Requirements

- Employed for at least 12 months (need not be consecutive)
- Worked at least 1,250 hours in the 12 months before leave
- Work at a location with 50+ employees within 75 miles

### Qualifying Reasons

- Birth and care of a newborn child
- Placement of a child for adoption or foster care
- Care for a spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition
- Employee's own serious health condition
- Qualifying exigency related to military service
- Military caregiver leave (up to 26 weeks)

### Application Process

1. Notify HR at least 30 days before foreseeable leave (as soon as possible for unforeseeable)
2. Complete the FMLA Request Form (HR Portal > Forms > Leave)
3. Obtain medical certification from healthcare provider (DOL Form WH-380)
4. Submit documentation to HR Operations within 15 calendar days
5. HR will issue an eligibility and designation notice within 5 business days

### During FMLA Leave

- Health insurance continues (employee must continue paying their share)
- PTO does not accrue during unpaid FMLA leave
- Intermittent leave is available when medically necessary
- Employee must provide fitness-for-duty certification before returning

Author: HR Generalist

Title: Sick leave policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What is the company sick leave policy for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Sick leave is provided separately from PTO for illness, medical appointments, and caring for ill family members.

### Sick Leave Allowance

- **Full-time employees**: 10 sick days per year (accrued at 0.83 days/month)
- **Part-time employees**: Prorated based on scheduled hours
- Unused sick leave carries over (up to 60-day maximum bank)
- Sick leave is not paid out upon separation

### Acceptable Uses

- Employee's own illness or injury
- Medical, dental, or vision appointments
- Care for an ill family member (spouse, child, parent)
- Mental health days
- Recovery from domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking

### Reporting Absences

1. Notify your manager before your scheduled start time (call, text, or email)
2. For absences of 3+ consecutive days, a doctor's note is required
3. Log the absence in the Time & Attendance system upon return
4. Extended illness (5+ days) may qualify for FMLA or short-term disability

### Patterns and Abuse

- Repeated patterns (e.g., every Monday/Friday) may trigger a review
- Suspected abuse will be addressed through the attendance policy
- Employees are encouraged to use sick leave appropriately — it's a benefit, not an entitlement to skip work

### Reporting Absences

1. Notify your manager before your scheduled start time (call, text, or email)
2. For absences of 3+ consecutive days, a doctor's note is required
3. Log the absence in the Time & Attendance system upon return
4. Extended illness (5+ days) may qualify for FMLA or short-term disability

### Patterns and Abuse

- Repeated patterns (e.g., every Monday/Friday) may trigger a review
- Suspected abuse will be addressed through the attendance policy
- Employees are encouraged to use sick leave appropriately — it's a benefit, not an entitlement to skip work
Author: HR Operations

Title: Parental leave benefits — new hire
Content:
## Question

What parental leave is available to employees in Engineering?

## Answer

The company offers paid parental leave beyond FMLA requirements.

### Paid Parental Leave

- **Birth Parent**: 16 weeks paid at 100% of base salary
- **Non-Birth Parent**: 8 weeks paid at 100% of base salary
- **Adoption/Foster**: 12 weeks paid at 100% of base salary
- Must be taken within 12 months of birth/placement
- Can be taken consecutively or intermittently (with manager approval)

### Eligibility

- Full-time employees who have completed 6 months of employment
- Both parents are eligible if both work for the company
- Applies to birth, adoption, and foster care placement

### How to Apply

1. Notify your manager and HR at least 30 days before expected leave
2. Complete the Parental Leave Request form on the HR Portal
3. Provide documentation:
   - Birth parent: Expected due date from healthcare provider
   - Adoption: Placement documentation from agency
   - Foster care: Placement letter from agency
4. HR will confirm leave dates and benefits continuation
5. Work with your manager on a transition plan

### During Parental Leave

- All benefits continue as if actively employed
- PTO continues to accrue
- Company-paid life insurance and disability continue
- 401(k) match applies to any pay received during leave
- Gradual return-to-work schedule available (part-time for 2 weeks upon return)
Author: HR Generalist

- All benefits continue as if actively employed
- PTO continues to accrue
- Company-paid life insurance and disability continue
- 401(k) match applies to any pay received during leave
- Gradual return-to-work schedule available (part-time for 2 weeks upon return)
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: Parental leave benefits — remote employee
Content:
## Question

What parental leave is available to employees in Research?

## Answer

The company offers paid parental leave beyond FMLA requirements.

### Paid Parental Leave

- **Birth Parent**: 16 weeks paid at 100% of base salary
- **Non-Birth Parent**: 8 weeks paid at 100% of base salary
- **Adoption/Foster**: 12 weeks paid at 100% of base salary
- Must be taken within 12 months of birth/placement
- Can be taken consecutively or intermittently (with manager approval)

### Eligibility

- Full-time employees who have completed 6 months of employment
- Both parents are eligible if both work for the company
- Applies to birth, adoption, and foster care placement

### How to Apply

1. Notify your manager and HR at least 30 days before expected leave
2. Complete the Parental Leave Request form on the HR Portal
3. Provide documentation:
   - Birth parent: Expected due date from healthcare provider
   - Adoption: Placement documentation from agency
   - Foster care: Placement letter from agency
4. HR will confirm leave dates and benefits continuation
5. Work with your manager on a transition plan

### During Parental Leave

- All benefits continue as if actively employed
- PTO continues to accrue
- Company-paid life insurance and disability continue
- 401(k) match applies to any pay received during leave
- Gradual return-to-work schedule available (part-time for 2 weeks upon return)

Author: HR Generalist

Title: Bereavement leave policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What bereavement leave is available when a Engineering employee experiences a death in the family?

## Answer

The company provides paid bereavement leave to support employees during times of loss.

### Bereavement Leave Allowance

| Relationship | Paid Days Off |
|-------------|--------------|
| Spouse/Domestic Partner | 5 days |
| Child (including stepchild) | 5 days |
| Parent (including stepparent) | 5 days |
| Sibling | 3 days |
| Grandparent | 3 days |
| Grandchild | 3 days |
| In-law (parent, sibling) | 3 days |
| Aunt, Uncle, Cousin, Niece, Nephew | 1 day |

### Additional Time

- Up to 2 additional days for travel over 500 miles
- PTO or unpaid leave may be used for additional time needed
- Extended bereavement for loss of spouse or child: up to 2 additional weeks unpaid

### Process

1. Notify your manager as soon as possible
2. Manager approves bereavement leave in the Time & Attendance system
3. No documentation required for up to 5 days
4. For extended bereavement, contact HR Operations

### Support Resources

- Employee Assistance Program (EAP): 800-555-0123 (24/7 counseling)
- Grief support groups: Listed on the EAP portal
- Flexible return-to-work arrangements available upon request
Author: Payroll Team

Title: Jury duty and civic leave — new hire
Content:
## Question

What is the policy for jury duty and civic leave for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company supports employees fulfilling their civic duties.

### Jury Duty

- **Pay**: Full pay for up to 10 days of jury duty per calendar year
- **Extended Service**: Beyond 10 days, the company pays the difference between jury duty pay and regular pay
- **Process**:
  1. Notify your manager as soon as you receive a jury summons
  2. Provide a copy of the summons to HR
  3. Report to work on any day you are excused early from jury service (if more than 4 hours remain in your shift)
  4. Provide proof of jury service upon return

### Voting Leave

- Up to 2 hours of paid time off to vote in federal, state, or local elections
- Must be taken at the beginning or end of your shift
- Notify your manager at least 2 days in advance

### Witness Duty

- Paid leave when subpoenaed as a witness (not as a party to the case)
- Same process as jury duty

### Other Civic Leave

- Volunteer election worker: 1 paid day per election year
- Emergency response volunteer (CERT, volunteer firefighter): up to 5 paid days per year

- **Pay**: Full pay for up to 10 days of jury duty per calendar year
- **Extended Service**: Beyond 10 days, the company pays the difference between jury duty pay and regular pay
- **Process**:
  1. Notify your manager as soon as you receive a jury summons
  2. Provide a copy of the summons to HR
  3. Report to work on any day you are excused early from jury service (if more than 4 hours remain in your shift)
  4. Provide proof of jury service upon return

### Voting Leave

- Up to 2 hours of paid time off to vote in federal, state, or local elections
- Must be taken at the beginning or end of your shift
- Notify your manager at least 2 days in advance

### Witness Duty

- Paid leave when subpoenaed as a witness (not as a party to the case)
- Same process as jury duty

### Other Civic Leave

- Volunteer election worker: 1 paid day per election year
- Emergency response volunteer (CERT, volunteer firefighter): up to 5 paid days per year
Author: Payroll Team

Title: How to request a leave of absence — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

An employee in Engineering needs an extended leave of absence. What are the options?

## Answer

The company offers several types of leave of absence beyond PTO and FMLA.

### Types of Leave

**Personal Leave of Absence**
- Up to 30 days unpaid
- Requires manager and HR VP approval
- Granted on a case-by-case basis
- Position held for duration of approved leave

**Medical Leave of Absence**
- Up to 6 months for serious medical conditions
- May run concurrently with FMLA and short-term disability
- Requires medical documentation
- Position held for FMLA-eligible period; best-effort after that

**Educational Leave**
- Up to 12 months for degree completion
- Must be relevant to current or future role
- Unpaid, but may be eligible for tuition reimbursement
- Position not guaranteed upon return

**Military Leave**
- In accordance with USERRA
- Full job protection and benefits continuation
- Pay differential for up to 12 months of active duty

### Application Process

1. Discuss the need with your manager
2. Submit a Leave of Absence Request via the HR Portal
3. Include supporting documentation (medical, educational enrollment, etc.)
4. HR reviews and responds within 5 business days
5. If approved, HR coordinates benefits continuation and return plan

### During Leave

- Health insurance: employee must pay full premium (employee + employer share) for unpaid leave beyond FMLA
- 401(k): contributions pause during unpaid leave
- PTO: does not accrue during unpaid leave
Author: Employee Relations Team
Tags: leave of absence,extended leave,unpaid leave,personal leave,leave,time-off

4. HR reviews and responds within 5 business days
5. If approved, HR coordinates benefits continuation and return plan

### During Leave

- Health insurance: employee must pay full premium (employee + employer share) for unpaid leave beyond FMLA
- 401(k): contributions pause during unpaid leave
- PTO: does not accrue during unpaid leave
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: How to request a leave of absence — contractor conversion
Content:
## Question

An employee in Research needs an extended leave of absence. What are the options?

## Answer

The company offers several types of leave of absence beyond PTO and FMLA.

### Types of Leave

**Personal Leave of Absence**
- Up to 30 days unpaid
- Requires manager and HR VP approval
- Granted on a case-by-case basis
- Position held for duration of approved leave

**Medical Leave of Absence**
- Up to 6 months for serious medical conditions
- May run concurrently with FMLA and short-term disability
- Requires medical documentation
- Position held for FMLA-eligible period; best-effort after that

**Educational Leave**
- Up to 12 months for degree completion
- Must be relevant to current or future role
- Unpaid, but may be eligible for tuition reimbursement
- Position not guaranteed upon return

**Military Leave**
- In accordance with USERRA
- Full job protection and benefits continuation
- Pay differential for up to 12 months of active duty

### Application Process

1. Discuss the need with your manager
2. Submit a Leave of Absence Request via the HR Portal
3. Include supporting documentation (medical, educational enrollment, etc.)
4. HR reviews and responds within 5 business days
5. If approved, HR coordinates benefits continuation and return plan

### During Leave

- Health insurance: employee must pay full premium (employee + employer share) for unpaid leave beyond FMLA
- 401(k): contributions pause during unpaid leave
- PTO: does not accrue during unpaid leave
Author: HR Operations

Title: Holiday schedule and floating holidays — new hire
Content:
## Question

What are the company holidays and how do floating holidays work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

### Company Holidays (2026)

| Holiday | Date |
|---------|------|
| New Year's Day | January 1 |
| Martin Luther King Jr. Day | January 19 |
| Presidents' Day | February 16 |
| Memorial Day | May 25 |
| Juneteenth | June 19 |
| Independence Day | July 3 (observed) |
| Labor Day | September 7 |
| Thanksgiving Day | November 26 |
| Day After Thanksgiving | November 27 |
| Christmas Eve | December 24 |
| Christmas Day | December 25 |
| New Year's Eve (half day) | December 31 |

### Floating Holidays

- All full-time employees receive **2 floating holidays** per year
- Must be used in the calendar year (no carryover)
- Schedule with your manager at least 1 week in advance
- Can be used for religious observances, cultural celebrations, or personal occasions

### Holiday Pay

- Non-exempt employees who work on a holiday receive 1.5x regular pay plus holiday pay
- Exempt employees who must work on a holiday receive a substitute day off
- Part-time employees receive holiday pay only for holidays falling on their scheduled workdays
Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: Short-term disability insurance — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does short-term disability (STD) coverage work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Short-term disability provides income replacement when an employee cannot work due to a non-work-related illness or injury.

### Coverage Details

- **Elimination Period**: 7 calendar days (sick leave can cover this period)
- **Benefit Amount**: 60% of base weekly salary (up to $2,500/week)
- **Duration**: Up to 26 weeks per disability event
- **Cost**: Fully paid by the company

### Eligible Conditions

- Illness or injury preventing performance of job duties
- Pregnancy and childbirth recovery
- Surgery and recovery
- Mental health conditions requiring treatment
- NOT covered: work-related injuries (use workers' compensation)

### How to File a Claim

1. Notify your manager and HR of your absence
2. If absence extends beyond 5 days, HR initiates the STD claim
3. Complete the employee statement form from the insurance carrier
4. Your physician completes the attending physician statement
5. Submit forms to the insurance carrier within 30 days of disability onset
6. The carrier reviews and provides a determination within 10 business days

### During STD Leave

- Health insurance continues (employee pays usual share)
- STD benefit is subject to applicable taxes
- FMLA runs concurrently if eligible
- Long-term disability (LTD) begins after 26 weeks if disability continues
Author: Payroll Team

1. Notify your manager and HR of your absence
2. If absence extends beyond 5 days, HR initiates the STD claim
3. Complete the employee statement form from the insurance carrier
4. Your physician completes the attending physician statement
5. Submit forms to the insurance carrier within 30 days of disability onset
6. The carrier reviews and provides a determination within 10 business days

### During STD Leave

- Health insurance continues (employee pays usual share)
- STD benefit is subject to applicable taxes
- FMLA runs concurrently if eligible
- Long-term disability (LTD) begins after 26 weeks if disability continues
Author: Learning & Development

Title: Short-term disability insurance — new hire
Content:
## Question

How does short-term disability (STD) coverage work for Finance employees?

## Answer

Short-term disability provides income replacement when an employee cannot work due to a non-work-related illness or injury.

### Coverage Details

- **Elimination Period**: 7 calendar days (sick leave can cover this period)
- **Benefit Amount**: 60% of base weekly salary (up to $2,500/week)
- **Duration**: Up to 26 weeks per disability event
- **Cost**: Fully paid by the company

### Eligible Conditions

- Illness or injury preventing performance of job duties

- NOT covered: work-related injuries (use workers' compensation)

Title: Employee discount and perks program — new hire
Content:
## Question

What employee discounts and perks are available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company offers a comprehensive perks program through multiple platforms.

### Wellness Benefits

- **Gym Membership Subsidy**: Up to $75/month reimbursement for gym or fitness app
- **Wellness Program**: Earn up to $500/year in wellness rewards for health activities
- **Mental Health**: 12 free counseling sessions per year through EAP
- **Ergonomic Assessment**: Free workstation evaluation and equipment (up to $500)

### Financial Perks

- **Student Loan Assistance**: $100/month employer contribution toward student loans
- **Financial Planning**: Free access to certified financial planner (4 sessions/year)
- **Legal Services**: Discounted legal plan at $15/month for estate planning, real estate, family law

### Lifestyle Discounts

- **Corporate Discount Portal**: Access at perks.company.com
  - Cell phone plans: 15-25% off major carriers
  - Auto/home insurance: 10% group discount
  - Travel: Preferred rates on hotels, car rentals, theme parks
  - Electronics: Special pricing on Dell, Apple, Samsung
  - Entertainment: Discounted movie tickets, streaming bundles

### Commuter Benefits

- **Pre-Tax Transit**: Up to $315/month pre-tax for public transit
- **Pre-Tax Parking**: Up to $315/month pre-tax for workplace parking
- **Bike to Work**: $50/month reimbursement for cycling commuters

### How to Access

1. Log in to perks.company.com with your company SSO
2. Browse categories or search for specific discounts
3. Follow redemption instructions for each offer
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: New employee first day checklist — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What should a new employee in Engineering expect on their first day?

## Answer

Welcome to the team! Here's what your first day looks like.

### Before You Arrive

- You'll receive a welcome email with parking information and building access instructions
- Your manager will send your first-week schedule
- IT will have your equipment ready at your workstation

### First Day Schedule

**8:30 AM - Arrival & Check-in**
1. Report to the front desk / reception
2. Receive your employee badge and parking pass
3. Complete any remaining paperwork (I-9 verification — bring required documents)

**9:00 AM - HR Orientation**
1. Company overview and culture
2. Benefits enrollment walkthrough
3. Review of HR policies and employee handbook
4. Sign required acknowledgment forms

**10:30 AM - IT Setup**
1. Laptop/workstation configuration
2. Email and system account activation
3. VPN and remote access setup
4. Security awareness overview

**12:00 PM - Lunch with Team**
- Your manager will coordinate a team lunch (company-paid first day)

**1:00 PM - Department Orientation**
1. Meet your Engineering team members
2. Tour of your work area and key facilities
3. Review of team goals and current projects

**3:00 PM - Manager 1:1**
1. Role expectations and 90-day goals
2. Regular meeting schedule
3. Questions and next steps

### Required Documents for I-9

Bring ONE from List A (e.g., US Passport) OR one from List B (e.g., Driver's License) AND one from List C (e.g., Social Security Card)
Author: HR Generalist

- IT will have your equipment ready at your workstation

### First Day Schedule

**8:30 AM - Arrival & Check-in**

2. Receive your employee badge and parking pass
3. Complete any remaining paperwork (I-9 verification — bring required documents)

**9:00 AM - HR Orientation**
1. Company overview and culture
2. Benefits enrollment walkthrough
3. Review of HR policies and employee handbook
4. Sign required acknowledgment forms

**10:30 AM - IT Setup**
1. Laptop/workstation configuration
2. Email and system account activation
3. VPN and remote access setup
4. Security awareness overview

**12:00 PM - Lunch with Team**
- Your manager will coordinate a team lunch (company-paid first day)

**1:00 PM - Department Orientation**
1. Meet your Procurement team members
2. Tour of your work area and key facilities
3. Review of team goals and current projects

**3:00 PM - Manager 1:1**
1. Role expectations and 90-day goals
2. Regular meeting schedule
3. Questions and next steps

### Required Documents for I-9

Bring ONE from List A (e.g., US Passport) OR one from List B (e.g., Driver's License) AND one from List C (e.g., Social Security Card)

Title: How to set up direct deposit — new hire
Content:
## Question

How does a new hire in Engineering set up direct deposit for payroll?

## Answer

Direct deposit setup should be completed within your first 3 days to ensure timely payment.

### Steps to Set Up Direct Deposit

1. Log in to the Employee Self-Service Portal at ess.company.com
2. Navigate to "Pay" > "Direct Deposit"
3. Click "Add Account"
4. Enter your bank information:
   - Bank name
   - Routing number (9 digits)
   - Account number
   - Account type (checking or savings)
5. Choose allocation:
   - **Full deposit**: 100% to one account
   - **Split deposit**: Divide between up to 3 accounts (by percentage or flat amount)
6. Upload a voided check or bank letter for verification
7. Submit

### Important Notes

- Changes take 1-2 pay cycles to take effect
- Until direct deposit is active, you'll receive a paper check mailed to your home address
- Pay schedule: Bi-weekly on Fridays (26 pay periods per year)
- You can update direct deposit at any time through the Self-Service Portal

### Troubleshooting

- **Rejected deposit**: Verify routing and account numbers; contact your bank for correct information
- **Partial deposit**: Check allocation percentages total 100%
- **Questions**: Contact Payroll Team at payroll@company.com
Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Resignation process and exit checklist — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What is the process when an employee in Engineering resigns?

## Answer

We appreciate advance notice and aim to make the transition smooth for everyone.

### Resignation Steps

1. **Notify Your Manager**: Have a conversation first, then follow up in writing
2. **Submit Formal Resignation**:
   - Email your manager and HR with your last working day
   - Standard notice period: 2 weeks (4 weeks for directors and above)
3. **HR Acknowledgment**: HR will confirm receipt and schedule an exit process

### Exit Checklist

**Week 1 of Notice Period**
- [ ] Knowledge transfer plan created with manager
- [ ] Document key processes and ongoing projects
- [ ] Introduce replacements or backups to key contacts
- [ ] Begin transitioning responsibilities

**Final Week**
- [ ] Return all company property:
  - Laptop and charger
  - Mobile phone (if company-issued)
  - Badge and access cards
  - Parking pass
  - Company credit card
  - Keys
- [ ] Remove personal files from company devices
- [ ] Update voicemail and set email auto-reply
- [ ] Complete exit interview with HR

### Exit Interview

- Conducted by HR (not your manager) — confidential
- Topics: reasons for leaving, feedback on culture, suggestions for improvement
- Typically 30-45 minutes, scheduled during your final week

### Final Pay and Benefits

- Final paycheck includes: remaining salary, accrued PTO payout, any expense reimbursements
- Benefits continue through the end of the month of separation
- COBRA continuation coverage information mailed within 14 days
- 401(k) remains in plan or can be rolled over
Author: Employee Relations Team

  - Parking pass
  - Company credit card
  - Keys
- [ ] Remove personal files from company devices
- [ ] Update voicemail and set email auto-reply
- [ ] Complete exit interview with HR

### Exit Interview

- Conducted by HR (not your manager) — confidential
- Topics: reasons for leaving, feedback on culture, suggestions for improvement
- Typically 30-45 minutes, scheduled during your final week

### Final Pay and Benefits

- Final paycheck includes: remaining salary, accrued PTO payout, any expense reimbursements
- Benefits continue through the end of the month of separation
- COBRA continuation coverage information mailed within 14 days
- 401(k) remains in plan or can be rolled over
Author: Talent Acquisition

Title: How to update personal information in HR systems — new hire
Content:
## Question

An employee in Engineering needs to update personal information (address, name, emergency contacts, etc.). How do they do this?

## Answer

Employees are responsible for keeping their personal information up to date.

### What Can Be Updated Self-Service

Log in to the Employee Self-Service Portal (ess.company.com):

**Contact Information**
- Home address
- Phone number(s)
- Personal email

**Emergency Contacts**
- Add, edit, or remove emergency contacts
- Include name, relationship, phone number

**Tax Withholdings**
- Federal W-4 changes
- State tax withholding changes

### Changes Requiring HR Assistance

**Legal Name Change**
1. Submit a name change request via HR Portal > Forms > Name Change
2. Attach supporting documentation:
   - Marriage certificate, divorce decree, or court order
   - Updated Social Security card
   - Updated driver's license
3. HR updates all systems within 3 business days
4. New badge issued if needed

**Marital Status Change**
- Notify HR for benefits eligibility changes
- You have 30 days from the event to adjust benefits

**Disability Status / Veteran Status**
- Voluntary self-identification can be updated at any time
- HR Portal > My Profile > EEO Self-Identification

### Important Reminders

- Address changes affect tax withholding, benefits, and emergency communications
- Update your address within 30 days of moving
- Benefits changes due to life events must be made within 30 days
Author: HR Compliance Team

Log in to the Employee Self-Service Portal (ess.company.com):

**Contact Information**
- Home address
- Phone number(s)
- Personal email

**Emergency Contacts**
- Add, edit, or remove emergency contacts
- Include name, relationship, phone number

**Tax Withholdings**
- Federal W-4 changes
- State tax withholding changes

### Changes Requiring HR Assistance

**Legal Name Change**
1. Submit a name change request via HR Portal > Forms > Name Change
2. Attach supporting documentation:
   - Marriage certificate, divorce decree, or court order
   - Updated Social Security card
   - Updated driver's license
3. HR updates all systems within 3 business days
4. New badge issued if needed

**Marital Status Change**
- Notify HR for benefits eligibility changes
- You have 30 days from the event to adjust benefits

**Disability Status / Veteran Status**
- Voluntary self-identification can be updated at any time
- HR Portal > My Profile > EEO Self-Identification

### Important Reminders

- Address changes affect tax withholding, benefits, and emergency communications
- Update your address within 30 days of moving
- Benefits changes due to life events must be made within 30 days
Author: HR Benefits Team

Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: Remote work and telecommuting policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What is the remote work policy for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company supports flexible work arrangements including remote and hybrid options.

### Work Arrangement Options

| Arrangement | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| On-Site | Full-time in office (5 days/week) |
| Hybrid | 3 days in office, 2 days remote |
| Remote | Primarily remote, quarterly on-site meetings |
| Fully Remote | 100% remote (approved roles only) |

### Eligibility

- Available after completing the 90-day probation period
- Must have satisfactory performance rating
- Role must be suitable for remote work (determined by manager and HR)
- Manager approval required

### How to Request

1. Discuss with your manager
2. Submit a Remote Work Agreement via the HR Portal
3. Agreement includes:
   - Proposed schedule and work location
   - Designated workspace description
   - Acknowledgment of remote work policies
4. Manager and HR review (5 business day response)
5. If approved, agreement is effective for 6 months (renewable)

### Remote Work Requirements

- Reliable internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps)
- Dedicated workspace with appropriate ergonomics
- Available during core hours (10 AM - 3 PM local time)
- Responsive on Slack/Teams during work hours
- Attend all required in-person meetings and events
- Follow all security policies (VPN, locked screen, no public Wi-Fi for sensitive data)

### Equipment

- Company provides: laptop, monitor, keyboard, mouse, headset
- $500 one-time home office stipend for remote/hybrid employees
- $75/month internet stipend for fully remote employees
Author: HR Operations

3. Agreement includes:
   - Proposed schedule and work location
   - Designated workspace description
   - Acknowledgment of remote work policies
4. Manager and HR review (5 business day response)
5. If approved, agreement is effective for 6 months (renewable)

### Remote Work Requirements

- Reliable internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps)
- Dedicated workspace with appropriate ergonomics
- Available during core hours (10 AM - 3 PM local time)
- Responsive on Slack/Teams during work hours
- Attend all required in-person meetings and events
- Follow all security policies (VPN, locked screen, no public Wi-Fi for sensitive data)

### Equipment

- Company provides: laptop, monitor, keyboard, mouse, headset
- $500 one-time home office stipend for remote/hybrid employees
- $75/month internet stipend for fully remote employees
Author: HR Operations

Title: Employee referral program — new hire
Content:
## Question

How does the employee referral program work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Employees are our best recruiters! The referral program rewards you for helping us find great talent.

### Referral Bonuses

| Position Level | Referral Bonus |
|---------------|---------------|
| Individual Contributor | $2,000 |
| Senior / Lead | $3,500 |
| Manager / Director | $5,000 |
| VP and above | $7,500 |
| Hard-to-fill roles (designated) | $10,000 |

### How to Submit a Referral

1. Log in to the Recruiting Platform at jobs.company.com/referrals
2. Search for open positions
3. Click "Refer Someone" on the job posting
4. Enter the candidate's name, email, phone, and resume
5. Add a note about how you know them and why they'd be a good fit
6. Submit — the candidate receives an automated invitation to apply

### Eligibility Rules

- All employees except HR, recruiting team, and hiring managers for the specific role
- Referral must be submitted before the candidate applies independently
- Candidate must be hired as a full-time employee
- Candidate must complete 90 days of employment for bonus payout

### Bonus Payment

- 50% paid after candidate's start date (next pay cycle)
- 50% paid after candidate completes 90 days
- Bonus is taxable income
- No limit on number of referrals per year

### Tips for Great Referrals

- Review the job description carefully before referring
- Let your referral know you're submitting them
- Provide context to the recruiter about the candidate's strengths

Title: I-9 verification and work authorization — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What are the I-9 requirements for new hires in Engineering?

## Answer

Federal law requires all employers to verify the identity and employment authorization of every person hired.

### Timeline

- **Section 1**: Employee completes on or before the first day of work
- **Section 2**: Employer completes within 3 business days of the employee's first day
- **Reverification**: Before expiration of work authorization (if applicable)

### Acceptable Documents

**List A — Establishes both identity AND employment authorization (need ONE):**
- U.S. Passport or Passport Card
- Permanent Resident Card (Green Card)
- Employment Authorization Document (EAD)
- Foreign passport with Form I-94

**List B — Establishes identity (need ONE, plus one from List C):**
- Driver's license or state ID
- School ID with photo
- Voter registration card
- U.S. military card

**List C — Establishes employment authorization (need ONE, plus one from List B):**
- Social Security card (unrestricted)
- Birth certificate
- Employment authorization from DHS

### Process

1. New hire completes Section 1 on Day 1 (via E-Verify portal)
2. HR examines original documents in person (copies are not acceptable)
3. HR completes Section 2 within 3 business days
4. Information is run through E-Verify

### Remote Hires

- Documents may be examined by an authorized representative
- Video examination is available through E-Verify's alternative procedure
- Original documents must still be presented (not copies)

### Important Notes

- Employers cannot specify which documents to present — employee's choice
- Expired documents are not acceptable (except for reverification of certain documents)
- I-9 forms retained for 3 years after hire or 1 year after termination (whichever is later)
Author: Talent Acquisition

- Original documents must still be presented (not copies)

### Important Notes

- Expired documents are not acceptable (except for reverification of certain documents)
- I-9 forms retained for 3 years after hire or 1 year after termination (whichever is later)

Title: Annual performance review process — new hire
Content:
## Question

How does the annual performance review process work in Engineering?

## Answer

Performance reviews are conducted annually with a mid-year check-in.

### Annual Review Timeline

| Phase | Timing | Activity |
|-------|--------|----------|
| Goal Setting | January | Set annual goals and development objectives |
| Mid-Year Check-in | June-July | Informal review of progress, adjust goals if needed |
| Self-Assessment | November 1-15 | Employee completes self-evaluation |
| Manager Review | November 15-30 | Manager completes evaluation and calibration |
| Calibration | December 1-10 | Leadership calibration sessions |
| Review Meetings | December 10-31 | Manager delivers review to employee |
| Merit/Bonus | March 1 | Compensation adjustments effective |

### Rating Scale

| Rating | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| 5 - Exceptional | Consistently far exceeds all expectations |
| 4 - Exceeds Expectations | Regularly exceeds expectations in key areas |
| 3 - Meets Expectations | Fully meets all role requirements |
| 2 - Needs Improvement | Falls short in some areas; development plan needed |
| 1 - Unsatisfactory | Fails to meet minimum expectations; PIP required |

### Self-Assessment Tips

- Reference specific accomplishments with measurable results
- Address each goal set at the beginning of the year
- Be honest about challenges and areas for growth
- Include feedback received from peers and stakeholders

### After the Review

- Both employee and manager sign the completed review
- Goals for the next year are drafted collaboratively
- Development plans are created for growth areas
- Reviews are stored in the HRIS system
Author: Employee Relations Team

| Merit/Bonus | March 1 | Compensation adjustments effective |

### Rating Scale

| Rating | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| 5 - Exceptional | Consistently far exceeds all expectations |
| 4 - Exceeds Expectations | Regularly exceeds expectations in key areas |
| 3 - Meets Expectations | Fully meets all role requirements |
| 2 - Needs Improvement | Falls short in some areas; development plan needed |
| 1 - Unsatisfactory | Fails to meet minimum expectations; PIP required |

### Self-Assessment Tips

- Reference specific accomplishments with measurable results
- Address each goal set at the beginning of the year
- Be honest about challenges and areas for growth
- Include feedback received from peers and stakeholders

### After the Review

- Both employee and manager sign the completed review
- Goals for the next year are drafted collaboratively
- Development plans are created for growth areas
- Reviews are stored in the HRIS system

Author: Talent Acquisition

Title: How to create a performance improvement plan — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

When and how should a PIP be created for an employee in Engineering?

## Answer

A Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) is a formal tool used when an employee consistently fails to meet expectations despite coaching and feedback.

### When to Use a PIP

- Employee has received a rating of 2 or below
- Documented performance concerns exist over 60+ days
- Informal coaching and feedback have not resolved the issues
- The employee's role is clear and expectations are documented

### PIP Process

**Step 1: Consult HR**
- Contact the Employee Relations Team before initiating
- HR reviews the situation, documentation, and confirms a PIP is appropriate
- HR provides the PIP template and guides the process

**Step 2: Draft the PIP**
Include:
- Specific performance deficiencies (with examples and dates)
- Clear, measurable improvement goals
- Resources and support provided (training, mentoring, tools)
- Timeline (typically 30, 60, or 90 days)
- Check-in schedule (weekly recommended)
- Consequences of not meeting improvement goals

**Step 3: Deliver the PIP**
- Meet with the employee (HR may attend)
- Review each element of the PIP
- Allow the employee to ask questions and provide input
- Both parties sign the document
- Employee receives a copy

**Step 4: Monitor and Document**
- Hold regular check-ins per the schedule
- Document progress in writing after each meeting
- Provide feedback — both positive progress and ongoing concerns

**Step 5: Outcome**
- **Successful**: Employee meets goals — PIP is closed, continued monitoring
- **Unsuccessful**: May result in demotion, role change, or termination

### Important Notes

- PIPs should not be used as a precursor to a predetermined termination
- The goal is genuine improvement and employee success
- All PIP documentation must be submitted to HR
Author: Employee Relations Team

Author: HR Generalist

Title: How to create a performance improvement plan — part-time employee
Content:
## Question

When and how should a PIP be created for an employee in Design?

## Answer

A Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) is a formal tool used when an employee consistently fails to meet expectations despite coaching and feedback.

### When to Use a PIP

- Employee has received a rating of 2 or below
- Documented performance concerns exist over 60+ days
- Informal coaching and feedback have not resolved the issues
- The employee's role is clear and expectations are documented

### PIP Process

**Step 1: Consult HR**
- Contact the Employee Relations Team before initiating
- HR reviews the situation, documentation, and confirms a PIP is appropriate
- HR provides the PIP template and guides the process

**Step 2: Draft the PIP**
Include:
- Specific performance deficiencies (with examples and dates)
- Clear, measurable improvement goals
- Resources and support provided (training, mentoring, tools)
- Timeline (typically 30, 60, or 90 days)
- Check-in schedule (weekly recommended)
- Consequences of not meeting improvement goals

**Step 3: Deliver the PIP**
- Meet with the employee (HR may attend)
- Review each element of the PIP
- Allow the employee to ask questions and provide input
- Both parties sign the document
- Employee receives a copy

**Step 4: Monitor and Document**
- Hold regular check-ins per the schedule
- Document progress in writing after each meeting
- Provide feedback — both positive progress and ongoing concerns

**Step 5: Outcome**
- **Successful**: Employee meets goals — PIP is closed, continued monitoring
- **Unsuccessful**: May result in demotion, role change, or termination

### Important Notes

- PIPs should not be used as a precursor to a predetermined termination
- The goal is genuine improvement and employee success
- All PIP documentation must be submitted to HR

Title: Goal setting with SMART objectives — new hire
Content:
## Question

How should Engineering employees set goals using the SMART framework?

## Answer

All annual goals should follow the SMART framework to ensure clarity and measurability.

### SMART Criteria

| Letter | Meaning | Question to Ask |
|--------|---------|-----------------|
| S | Specific | What exactly will be accomplished? |
| M | Measurable | How will success be quantified? |
| A | Achievable | Is it realistic with available resources? |
| R | Relevant | Does it align with team and company objectives? |
| T | Time-bound | What is the deadline? |

### Goal Categories

Each employee should set 4-6 goals across these categories:
- **Business Goals** (2-3): Directly tied to team/company objectives
- **Development Goals** (1-2): Skills growth, certifications, or career advancement
- **Cultural Goals** (1): Contribution to team culture, mentoring, or company values

### Examples for Engineering

**Poor Goal**: "Improve customer satisfaction"
**SMART Goal**: "Increase customer satisfaction score from 82% to 90% by Q3 2026 by implementing a new feedback loop process and responding to all tickets within 4 hours"

**Poor Goal**: "Get better at leadership"
**SMART Goal**: "Complete the Emerging Leaders certification program by June 2026 and lead at least 2 cross-functional projects in H2"

### Process

1. Draft goals in the Performance Management Tool by January 31
2. Review and finalize with your manager by February 15
3. Goals are locked and visible to manager and HR
4. Update progress quarterly
5. Refer to goals during mid-year and annual reviews

### Modifying Goals

- Goals can be adjusted mid-year with manager approval
- Changes should be documented with the reason for modification
- Notify HR if a goal is being removed entirely
Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: Goal setting with SMART objectives — remote employee
Content:
## Question

How should Research employees set goals using the SMART framework?

## Answer

All annual goals should follow the SMART framework to ensure clarity and measurability.

### SMART Criteria

| Letter | Meaning | Question to Ask |
|--------|---------|-----------------|
| S | Specific | What exactly will be accomplished? |
| M | Measurable | How will success be quantified? |
| A | Achievable | Is it realistic with available resources? |
| R | Relevant | Does it align with team and company objectives? |
| T | Time-bound | What is the deadline? |

### Goal Categories

Each employee should set 4-6 goals across these categories:
- **Business Goals** (2-3): Directly tied to team/company objectives
- **Development Goals** (1-2): Skills growth, certifications, or career advancement
- **Cultural Goals** (1): Contribution to team culture, mentoring, or company values

### Examples for Research

**Poor Goal**: "Improve customer satisfaction"
**SMART Goal**: "Increase customer satisfaction score from 82% to 90% by Q3 2026 by implementing a new feedback loop process and responding to all tickets within 4 hours"

**Poor Goal**: "Get better at leadership"
**SMART Goal**: "Complete the Emerging Leaders certification program by June 2026 and lead at least 2 cross-functional projects in H2"

### Process

1. Draft goals in the Performance Management Tool by January 31
2. Review and finalize with your manager by February 15
3. Goals are locked and visible to manager and HR
4. Update progress quarterly
5. Refer to goals during mid-year and annual reviews

### Modifying Goals

- Goals can be adjusted mid-year with manager approval
- Changes should be documented with the reason for modification
- Notify HR if a goal is being removed entirely
Author: Payroll Team

Title: How to give and receive feedback — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What are the best practices for giving and receiving feedback in Engineering?

## Answer

Feedback is a core part of our performance culture. Here's how to do it effectively.

### Giving Feedback

**The SBI Model (Situation-Behavior-Impact)**

1. **Situation**: Describe the specific context
   - "In yesterday's client meeting..."
2. **Behavior**: Describe the observable behavior (not personality)
   - "...you interrupted the client twice while they were explaining their concerns..."
3. **Impact**: Describe the effect
   - "...which made it difficult to fully understand their needs and may have seemed dismissive."

### Tips for Giving Feedback

- Give it promptly (within 48 hours of the event)
- Be specific — avoid vague statements like "you need to communicate better"
- Focus on behavior, not character
- Balance positive and constructive feedback (but don't sandwich — be direct)
- Offer suggestions for improvement
- Have the conversation privately (praise in public, coach in private)

### Receiving Feedback

- Listen without interrupting or becoming defensive
- Ask clarifying questions: "Can you give me a specific example?"
- Thank the person for sharing
- Take time to reflect before responding
- Create an action plan if needed
- Follow up to show you've acted on the feedback

### Formal Feedback Channels

- **360 Reviews**: Conducted annually alongside performance reviews
  - Self, manager, 3-5 peers, and direct reports (if applicable)
  - Anonymous peer feedback
  - Managed through the Performance Management Tool
- **Continuous Feedback**: Use the "Give Kudos" or "Request Feedback" features in the tool anytime
- **Skip-Level Meetings**: Optional quarterly meetings with your manager's manager

### Creating a Feedback Culture

- Request feedback regularly — don't wait for reviews
- Recognize good work publicly in team channels
- Model vulnerability by sharing your own development areas
Author: HR Benefits Team

Author: Learning & Development

Title: How to give and receive feedback — Procurement department
Content:
## Question

What are the best practices for giving and receiving feedback in Procurement?

## Answer

Feedback is a core part of our performance culture. Here's how to do it effectively.

### Giving Feedback

**The SBI Model (Situation-Behavior-Impact)**

1. **Situation**: Describe the specific context
   - "In yesterday's client meeting..."
2. **Behavior**: Describe the observable behavior (not personality)
   - "...you interrupted the client twice while they were explaining their concerns..."
3. **Impact**: Describe the effect
   - "...which made it difficult to fully understand their needs and may have seemed dismissive."

### Tips for Giving Feedback

- Give it promptly (within 48 hours of the event)
- Be specific — avoid vague statements like "you need to communicate better"
- Focus on behavior, not character
- Balance positive and constructive feedback (but don't sandwich — be direct)
- Offer suggestions for improvement
- Have the conversation privately (praise in public, coach in private)

### Receiving Feedback

- Listen without interrupting or becoming defensive
- Ask clarifying questions: "Can you give me a specific example?"
- Thank the person for sharing
- Take time to reflect before responding
- Create an action plan if needed
- Follow up to show you've acted on the feedback

### Formal Feedback Channels

- **360 Reviews**: Conducted annually alongside performance reviews
  - Self, manager, 3-5 peers, and direct reports (if applicable)
  - Anonymous peer feedback
  - Managed through the Performance Management Tool
- **Continuous Feedback**: Use the "Give Kudos" or "Request Feedback" features in the tool anytime
- **Skip-Level Meetings**: Optional quarterly meetings with your manager's manager

### Creating a Feedback Culture

- Request feedback regularly — don't wait for reviews
- Recognize good work publicly in team channels
- Model vulnerability by sharing your own development areas

Title: Promotion criteria and process — new hire
Content:
## Question

What is the promotion process for employees in Engineering?

## Answer

Promotions recognize sustained high performance and readiness for increased responsibility.

### Promotion Criteria

1. **Consistent Performance**: Rating of 4+ for at least 2 consecutive review cycles
2. **Demonstrated Competency**: Already performing at the next level for 3-6 months
3. **Business Need**: An opening or expanded scope exists
4. **Manager Recommendation**: Supported by direct manager and skip-level
5. **Peer Feedback**: Positive 360 feedback and stakeholder endorsement

### Promotion Process

| Step | Owner | Timeline |
|------|-------|----------|
| Manager identifies candidate | Manager | Ongoing |
| Manager discusses with HR | Manager + HR | 2 weeks before submission |
| Promotion packet submitted | Manager | By March 1 or September 1 |
| Leadership review and calibration | VP + HR | Within 2 weeks |
| Approval decision | SVP / C-level | Within 1 week |
| Employee notification | Manager | Within 3 business days of approval |
| Effective date | HR | Next pay period |

### Promotion Packet Includes

- Current role and proposed new role
- Justification with specific examples of next-level work
- Performance history (ratings, key accomplishments)
- Development areas and plan for growth in new role
- Compensation recommendation (new salary, equity refresh if applicable)

### Promotion Cycles

- Two formal cycles per year: April 1 and October 1
- Off-cycle promotions are rare and require VP+ approval
- Lateral moves (same level, different role) can occur anytime

### If Not Promoted

- Manager should have a transparent conversation about gaps
- Create a development plan with clear milestones
- Revisit at the next cycle
- Consider cross-functional exposure or stretch assignments
Author: HR Operations

4. **Manager Recommendation**: Supported by direct manager and skip-level
5. **Peer Feedback**: Positive 360 feedback and stakeholder endorsement

### Promotion Process

| Step | Owner | Timeline |
|------|-------|----------|
| Manager identifies candidate | Manager | Ongoing |
| Manager discusses with HR | Manager + HR | 2 weeks before submission |
| Promotion packet submitted | Manager | By March 1 or September 1 |
| Leadership review and calibration | VP + HR | Within 2 weeks |
| Approval decision | SVP / C-level | Within 1 week |
| Employee notification | Manager | Within 3 business days of approval |
| Effective date | HR | Next pay period |

### Promotion Packet Includes

- Current role and proposed new role
- Justification with specific examples of next-level work
- Performance history (ratings, key accomplishments)
- Development areas and plan for growth in new role
- Compensation recommendation (new salary, equity refresh if applicable)

### Promotion Cycles

- Two formal cycles per year: April 1 and October 1
- Off-cycle promotions are rare and require VP+ approval
- Lateral moves (same level, different role) can occur anytime

### If Not Promoted

- Manager should have a transparent conversation about gaps
- Create a development plan with clear milestones
- Revisit at the next cycle
- Consider cross-functional exposure or stretch assignments
Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: How to file a workplace harassment complaint — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

An employee in Engineering wants to report workplace harassment. What is the process?

## Answer

The company has zero tolerance for harassment of any kind. All reports are taken seriously and investigated promptly.

### What Constitutes Harassment

- Unwelcome conduct based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or veteran status
- Conduct that creates an intimidating, hostile, or offensive work environment
- Includes verbal, physical, visual, and electronic (email, chat, social media) conduct

### How to Report

**Option 1: Direct Report**
- Report to your manager, any manager, or HR directly
- Email: employeerelations@company.com
- Phone: 800-555-0456

**Option 2: Anonymous Hotline**
- Ethics Hotline: 800-555-0789
- Online: ethics.company.com
- Reports are confidential and can be anonymous

**Option 3: EEOC**
- Employees always have the right to file a complaint with the EEOC
- This does not replace or prevent internal reporting

### Investigation Process

1. HR acknowledges receipt within 24 hours
2. Interim protective measures implemented if needed (separation of parties)
3. Trained investigator assigned within 48 hours
4. Interviews conducted with complainant, respondent, and witnesses
5. Evidence reviewed (emails, messages, security footage, etc.)
6. Findings and determination made within 30 days
7. Appropriate action taken (up to and including termination)
8. Complainant notified of resolution (within confidentiality limits)

### Anti-Retaliation

- Retaliation against anyone who reports or participates in an investigation is strictly prohibited
- Retaliation is a separate, terminable offense
- Report suspected retaliation immediately through any channel above

Author: Learning & Development

Title: Workplace accommodation requests under ADA — new hire
Content:
## Question

How does an employee in Engineering request a reasonable accommodation under the ADA?

## Answer

The company is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified employees with disabilities.

### What Is a Reasonable Accommodation

- A modification to the job, work environment, or work process
- Enables a qualified person with a disability to perform essential job functions
- Examples: modified schedule, ergonomic equipment, assistive technology, telework, reassignment

### Request Process

1. **Initiate Request**
   - Notify your manager or HR that you need an accommodation
   - Formal written request is not required (verbal is sufficient)
   - Submit the Accommodation Request Form via HR Portal (recommended for documentation)

2. **Interactive Process**
   - HR schedules a meeting with you to discuss:
     - Nature of the limitation
     - Essential functions affected
     - Possible accommodations
   - You may be asked to provide medical documentation from your healthcare provider

3. **Evaluation**
   - HR evaluates proposed accommodations
   - Consults with your manager on operational feasibility
   - May propose alternative accommodations that meet the same need

4. **Decision**
   - HR provides a written response within 10 business days
   - If approved, implementation timeline provided
   - If denied, explanation of undue hardship and alternative options offered

5. **Implementation & Follow-Up**
   - Accommodation is documented and implemented
   - Follow-up review at 30 and 90 days to assess effectiveness
   - Adjustments made as needed

### Confidentiality

- Medical information is kept confidential in a separate file
- Only those with a need to know are informed (manager may know the accommodation but not the diagnosis)
- Medical records are not part of the personnel file

### Temporary Accommodations

- Available for temporary conditions (pregnancy, recovery from surgery)
- Same request process applies
- Duration based on medical provider recommendation

- Enables a qualified person with a disability to perform essential job functions
- Examples: modified schedule, ergonomic equipment, assistive technology, telework, reassignment

### Request Process

1. **Initiate Request**
   - Notify your manager or HR that you need an accommodation
   - Formal written request is not required (verbal is sufficient)
   - Submit the Accommodation Request Form via HR Portal (recommended for documentation)

2. **Interactive Process**
   - HR schedules a meeting with you to discuss:
     - Nature of the limitation
     - Essential functions affected
     - Possible accommodations
   - You may be asked to provide medical documentation from your healthcare provider

3. **Evaluation**
   - HR evaluates proposed accommodations
   - Consults with your manager on operational feasibility
   - May propose alternative accommodations that meet the same need

4. **Decision**
   - HR provides a written response within 10 business days
   - If approved, implementation timeline provided
   - If denied, explanation of undue hardship and alternative options offered

5. **Implementation & Follow-Up**
   - Accommodation is documented and implemented
   - Follow-up review at 30 and 90 days to assess effectiveness
   - Adjustments made as needed

### Confidentiality

- Medical information is kept confidential in a separate file
- Only those with a need to know are informed (manager may know the accommodation but not the diagnosis)
- Medical records are not part of the personnel file

### Temporary Accommodations

- Available for temporary conditions (pregnancy, recovery from surgery)
- Same request process applies
- Duration based on medical provider recommendation

Author: Payroll Team

Title: Employee Assistance Program overview — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What services does the Employee Assistance Program (EAP) provide for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The EAP provides free, confidential support for personal and work-related challenges.

### Services Available

**Counseling (Free)**
- 12 sessions per issue per year at no cost
- Licensed counselors specializing in:
  - Stress and anxiety
  - Depression
  - Relationship and family issues
  - Grief and loss
  - Substance abuse
  - Work-life balance
- In-person, phone, or video sessions available

**Work-Life Services**
- Childcare resources and referrals
- Eldercare planning and support
- Adoption resources
- Pet care referrals
- Moving and relocation assistance

**Financial Services**
- Budgeting and debt management counseling
- Tax preparation assistance
- Student loan consultation
- Retirement planning basics

**Legal Services**
- Free 30-minute consultation with an attorney
- 25% discount on retained services
- Coverage: family law, estate planning, real estate, consumer issues

### How to Access

- **Phone**: 800-555-0123 (24/7, 365 days)
- **Online**: eap.company.com
- **App**: Download "MyEAP" from any app store
- **Crisis Line**: Same number, press 1 for immediate support

### Confidentiality

- EAP is completely confidential
- Your employer does not know who uses the service
- No information is shared with HR or your manager
- Exception: imminent danger to self or others (legal reporting obligation)

### Family Members

- EAP services are available to all household members at no cost
- This includes spouse/partner, dependents, and anyone living in your home
Author: HR Generalist
